name: Ricobayo Reservoir Loop
name_original: De Tierra Alba por los pueblos de la ribera del Río Aliste — circular por Vegalatrave
description: This difficult 45.3-mile route near Vegalatrave follows the Río Aliste through villages and historical sites, best suited for mountain bikin
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Carbajales De Alba traverse a diverse landscape characterized by a mosaic of fields and hills, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features significant natural elements, including large reservoirs and the Esla River. Notable natural features include the Ricobayo Dam and Reservoir, which provides views from its vast structure within a deep river gorge. The area's varied gradients and scenic backdrops make it suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Carbajales De Alba The most popular road cycling route is Ricobayo Reservoir and Tierra de Alba Loop, a 30.1 miles (48.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the reservoir area and the surrounding Tierra de Alba region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ricobayo Reservoir Loop, a moderate 41.8 miles (67.3 km) path. This route offers extensive views of the Ricobayo Reservoir and its natural surroundings. Local road cyclists also love the Ricobayo Reservoir Loop, a 45.3 miles (73.0 km) trail leading through the reservoir area, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. This difficult route provides a challenging experience with significant elevation gain. Road cycling around Carbajales De Alba is defined by its mosaic of fields, rolling hills, and large reservoirs, including the Ricobayo Reservoir. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Carbajales De Alba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Carbajales De Alba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Carbajales De Alba?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Carbajales De Alba, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 200 times.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Carbajales De Alba?
A: The road cycling routes in Carbajales De Alba primarily range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 18 moderate routes and 11 challenging routes, providing options for both intermediate and experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Carbajales De Alba?
A: Road cycling in Carbajales De Alba takes you through a diverse landscape characterized by a mosaic of fields and rolling hills. You'll also encounter significant natural features like large reservoirs, most notably the Ricobayo Reservoir, and the Esla River, offering varied gradients and scenic backdrops.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or natural features to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region boasts several impressive landmarks and natural features. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Dam&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy panoramic views of the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and cross the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdoradas Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5714203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro de la Nave&lt;/a&gt; is also a point of interest.

Q: Can I cycle past the Ricobayo Dam?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature in the area, and many routes are designed to offer spectacular views of its vast concrete structure within the deep river gorge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38143849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir – Ricobayo Dam loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes this impressive landmark.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadozos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a known natural attraction in the vicinity of Carbajales De Alba. While not directly on every road cycling route, it's a highlight that can be incorporated into your cycling plans.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 11 difficult routes available. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369586580/ricobayo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 73 km and features significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Carbajales De Alba are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369316545/ricobayo-reservoir-and-tierra-de-alba-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir and Tierra de Alba Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369324841/ricobayo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers numerous scenic viewpoints, particularly around the Ricobayo Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Dam&lt;/a&gt; itself provides spectacular views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdoradas Bridge&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent vistas of the reservoir and its surrounding environment.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Carbajales De Alba?
A: The road cycling routes in Carbajales De Alba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the reservoirs, and the opportunities for both moderate and challenging rides.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes near Carbajales De Alba?
A: Yes, the area is known for offering longer routes. For instance, the region has been a stage in the Zamora Cycling Tour, indicating its suitability for competitive and long-distance road cycling. You can find routes up to 73 km, such as the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369586580/ricobayo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Carbajales De Alba?
A: While specific seasonal weather details are not provided, regions in Spain with a 'mosaic of fields and hills' typically offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn. These seasons generally provide milder temperatures and less extreme weather, making them ideal for exploring the varied terrain.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for road cycling?
faqs.answer: Despite being listed for racebikes, this route is not suitable for road cycling. It is primarily designed for mountain biking (BTT) and consists entirely of unpaved surfaces like cart tracks, forest tracks, and natural paths. There are no sections on paved roads, and some parts can be rocky and slippery, which is not ideal for road bikes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Río Aliste riverbank route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and unpaved, featuring cart tracks, forest tracks, and natural paths with abundant vegetation. You'll encounter rocky and potentially slippery surfaces, especially in the higher course of the river. Some sections also have traditional paved paths under granite walls.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route by mountain bike?
faqs.answer: For mountain bikers, the route is approximately 65.9 km long and is estimated to take around 6 hours to complete. This duration is for mountain biking, as the trail is not suitable for road cycling.

faqs.question: What are some notable sights or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque scenery along the Río Aliste, with alders, poplars, and ash trees. You'll pass traditional flour mills, dovecotes, waterwheels, and stone bridges. Highlights include the villages of Losacino, Gallegos del Río, and Perilla de Castro, as well as the Vegalatrave Church. You might also spot historical elements like the 12th-century Alba Castle and the ruins of Fort San Carlos.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder and the vegetation is lush. Summers can be very dry, leading to lower river levels, while winters can bring higher water levels due to tributaries. Avoid periods of heavy rain, as unpaved sections can become very muddy and slippery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Tierra Alba or Vegalatrave?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, as a circular route passing through villages like Tierra Alba and Vegalatrave, you can generally find parking within or on the outskirts of these settlements. Look for public parking spaces or areas suitable for leaving a vehicle safely before starting your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Río Aliste riverbank route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the varied terrain, including rocky and potentially slippery sections, ensure your dog is accustomed to such conditions. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock, and be mindful of other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride this trail. It is a public route through the natural and rural landscape of the Aliste region.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several local paths, including sections of the Cañada de Losacio, Calle Barrero, and Calle Era Tejar. These intersections are generally short, making up about 1% of the route each.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered of medium difficulty for mountain biking. The varied unpaved terrain, including forest tracks and natural paths, requires off-road cycling skills. It is 100% cyclable, meaning you shouldn't need to dismount and walk for mountain bikes.

faqs.question: Are there any places to find food or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several traditional villages, such as Losacino, Gallegos del Río, and Perilla de Castro. While specific cafes or pubs are not guaranteed in every village, these settlements offer opportunities to find local establishments for refreshments or to replenish supplies.
